greeting: The shopper turns a tin cup over in both hands, testing the rim for dents and the price for lies.
shrug: "I only came for salt and lamp oil."
deflect: "Ask the counter. I am only looking."

entries.text: "No name worth writing down. I keep a house upriver and a list longer than my purse."
"Buying what the road forgot to bring and what the canal soaked getting here."
"Prices rise whenever the mountain road is bad. Lately they rise even on clear days."
"Quarry men buy lamp oil now more than powder. That seems backward to me."
